plan_metadata.name: Feature Check DA New Env AgentScope

description.other: to check new environment has new env Agent_Scope

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown menu under "New Project" to initiate the creation of an agent by declaring instructions for Microsoft 365 Copilot.
Click the dropdown option "Add an Action" from the "Add an action to your declarative agent" menu in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog.
Click on the dropdown option "Start with a New API" in the "Create an Action" window within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "None" option within the "Select an authentication type" dropdown menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "TypeScript" option in the programming language dropdown menu to select it.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown within the "Workspace Folder" setup dialog to select the folder path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects."
Click on the file named `m365agents.local.yml` in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Create New Environment" button in the "ENVIRONMENT" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type text: 'test' into the input field labeled 'New environment name' in the popup interface within Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the new environment name input ("test") in the Visual Studio Code prompt modal.
Click the "Explorer" tab icon (tooltip: "Explorer (Ctrl+Shift+E)") in the Visual Studio Code sidebar at coordinates (17, 53) to expand or focus the Explorer panel.
Click on the directory named "env" within the Explorer panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the file ".env.test" in the Explorer panel within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion  'AGENT_SCOPE=personal' line with the '.env.test' file in the Visual Studio Code editor interface.
